ques: Read the latest health-related news article published on BBC News and summarize the key points discussed.
answer: The latest health article, "'Pregorexia': The hidden struggle of eating disorders facing pregnant women" (BBC Health, 16 Jun 2026), reports that ~1 in 20 pregnant women (and ~13% postpartum) suffer eating disorders — a largely hidden, under-screened problem. Pregnancy is a "perfect storm" of hormonal change, weight gain and loss of control that can trigger or relapse anorexia/bulimia, nearly doubling complications and endangering both mother (nutrient deficiency, bone/muscle loss) and baby (miscarriage, low birth weight, premature birth, developmental risks). Cases overlap with normal pregnancy symptoms and go undetected amid stigma and judgment. Experts say it's also a pivotal chance to recover, urging non-judgmental, multidisciplinary care, less focus on weight numbers, and patients confiding in trusted people — "nobody recovers alone."
